sound_type: deep grunting growl -- low, grunting, growling vocalizations at colony. Deep and resonant. Intermittent. Burrow and surface. E=4
ecosystem: Rocky offshore islands. Open ocean for feeding. Burrow nesting on islands with deep soil. Southern edge of significant breeding in PNW.
season: Summer breeder on PNW islands. Present offshore spring through fall. Winters at sea.

coordinates.description: Tufted Puffin on PNW offshore islands. Protection Island, Destruction Island. Washington State Endangered. >90% decline.

taxonomy_note.description: THIRD ALCIDAE. Continuing auk era (174-176). The PNW's most iconic seabird. Washington State Endangered -- over 90% population decline. New genus: Fratercula (puffins).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Alcidae: Pigeon Guillemot (174), Rhinoceros Auklet (175), Tufted Puffin (176).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Fratercula from Latin 'fraterculus' (little brother/friar) -- referring to the bird's dark plumage resembling a friar's robes. cirrhata from Latin 'cirrhatus' (curly/tufted) -- the golden tufts. Tufted Puffin: the golden tufts behind the eyes.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Fratercula -- puffins. 3 species worldwide. Tufted Puffin is the largest. Pacific distribution. Sister to Cerorhinca (Rhinoceros Auklet).
taxonomy_note.order_note: Charadriiformes. Alcidae continuing from degrees 174-175. Third consecutive entry.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Third Alcidae. First Fratercula. Iconic species. Conservation crisis. Sister to Rhinoceros Auklet.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 300-1500 Hz. Very low for a seabird.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual growls 0.5-2 seconds. Less continuous than Rhinoceros Auklet.
song_metrics.note_structure: Deep grunts and growls: 'grrrrr-ah' and 'uuuurr.' Low-pitched, resonant. Intermittent.
song_metrics.note_types: deep growl (primary -- colony/burrow), bill clacking (defense), soft grunting (mate communication), chick begging (from burrow).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Individual growls. No structured phrases. Colony produces intermittent collective sound.
song_metrics.timbre: Deep, guttural, resonant. Lower than Rhinoceros Auklet. Felt more than heard at distance.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Deep and resonant. Colony effect present. Less persistent than auklet.
song_metrics.tempo: Intermittent. Individual growls punctuated rather than continuous.
song_metrics.context: Colony interactions, burrow defense, mate communication. Breeding islands. Day and night.
song_metrics.key_property: DEEP GRUNTING GROWL -- colony, low-frequency, resonant. ICON IN CRISIS. >90% WA decline. State Endangered. Orange bill + golden tufts + white face. E=4. v1.49.316.

plumage.overall: Breeding: entirely black body, white face, long golden-yellow tufts sweeping back from eyes. Massive orange-red bill with olive/yellow base markings. Non-breeding: face dark, tufts absent, bill smaller/duller.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: White face + golden tufts + massive orange bill (breeding). Unmistakable. The most recognizable seabird in the PNW.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Dramatic. Breeding: white face, golden tufts, bright bill. Non-breeding: dark face, no tufts, reduced bill. Bill partly shed after breeding.
plumage.flight_pattern: Fast, direct, low over water. Short stiff wings. Rapid wingbeats. Typical alcid flight. Stocky silhouette.

physical_description.overall: Length: 36-41 cm. Weight: 660-900g. Bill: massive, laterally compressed, bright orange-red (breeding). Largest puffin species. Stocky, powerful build.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Massive orange bill. White face. Golden tufts. Dark body. Largest Alcidae in engine.
physical_description.field_id: Stocky dark seabird with white face, golden tufts, and enormous orange bill (breeding). At sea: dark, stocky, rapid wingbeats. Increasingly rare in Washington.

nodes.title: Conservation Crisis -- Over 90% Decline
nodes.description: Washington State Endangered (2015). >90% decline since 1970s. Ocean warming, prey collapse (sand lance, herring), Bald Eagle predation, introduced predators. San Juans functionally extinct. Most severe seabird conservation crisis in PNW.

nodes.title: The Icon -- Most Recognizable Seabird
nodes.description: White face, golden tufts, massive orange bill. Known by everyone. Postcards, t-shirts, children's books. Charismatic megafauna of the seabird world. Peak visibility. The icon endangered.

nodes.title: Fish Carrying -- 5-20 Fish Crosswise
nodes.description: Carries multiple fish crosswise in bill. Tongue presses fish against mandible serrations. Dives to 60+ meters (deepest Alcidae in engine). Prey availability crisis reduces chick feeding.

nodes.title: Prey Collapse -- Ocean Warming
nodes.description: Warming sea surface temperatures reduce forage fish. Sand lance, herring, smelt declining. Phenological mismatch compounds the problem. Primary driver of puffin decline.

cross_references.relationship: ALCIDAE PAIR! Both burrow nesters. Both offshore island breeders. Both nocturnal colony visitors. Sister genera (Fratercula/Cerorhinca). Auklet: stable. Puffin: endangered.
ALCIDAE TRIO! Three consecutive Alcidae (174-176). Urban crevice nester (174) to offshore burrow nester (176). Three modes of auk life. Stable to endangered.
CONSERVATION! Both face habitat/prey threats. Bittern: wetland loss. Puffin: ocean warming + prey collapse. Different ecosystems, same pressure.
ROCKY COAST! Both on rocky marine habitat. Oystercatcher: intertidal. Puffin: offshore island. Both sensitive to disturbance.
